summary: Thailand public-only skeleton for DBD DataWarehouse+ and data.go.th source planning. It does not expose automated DBD lookup, ownership/financial enrichment, or hosted bulk monitoring until DBD terms, CAPTCHA, rate-limit, caching, and redistribution constraints are approved.

auth.notes: The skeleton has no live credentialed adapter. Any future DBD or licensed intermediary integration must define TH_API_* credentials only after source terms are approved.

licensing.notes: DBD DataWarehouse+ appears to provide public search surfaces, but automated hosted use, rate limits, CAPTCHA behavior, caching, bulk use, and redistribution require explicit review before a live adapter.

freshness.refreshCadence: manual source review until DBD automated-use terms are approved

publicDataLimits.message: This pack documents Thailand source boundaries and does not perform a live DBD company lookup.
Do not automate, cache, bulk monitor, or redistribute DBD-derived company records without approved source terms and anti-automation review.
Outputs are public-source planning artifacts and not legal, tax, credit, investment, or licensed compliance advice.

tools.name: th_dbd_public_brief
tools.purpose: Document Thailand DBD public-search availability, data.go.th discovery paths, and known automation/licensing gaps for a company name or registration identifier.

publicDataLimits.message: The skeleton may cite DBD source boundaries but must not return automated DBD company, ownership, financial, or filing rows.

expectedGaps.message: DBD live lookup requires source-terms, rate-limit, CAPTCHA, caching, and redistribution review before automation.

contributionNotes: Review DBD and data.go.th terms for automated access, CAPTCHA handling, caching, attribution, rate limits, and commercial hosted use before adding adapters.
Use TH_API_* environment variables for future credentials and keep them server-side only.
Add recorded-source tests for manual DBD examples, no-match, ambiguous-match, upstream failure, stale source metadata, and blocked automation paths.
Preserve provenance, freshness, gaps, and limits in every output.
